user_query:
I’m preparing a seminar that challenges teleological readings of early Japan and need a quick, verifiable health check of my local project.

Please inspect the workspace using only local files provided here:
- Content to scan: all .md files under notes/ and drafts/
- Cross-reference: sources/citations.json (citation keys)
- Linked resources: files referenced by Markdown links or image syntax that use relative paths starting with "../" or "./"

Produce two deliverables:
1) Create reports/project_status_ancient_japan.md with:
   - An Executive summary (100–150 words) explaining overall readiness to support my critique and what gaps (TODOs, missing citations, broken local links) need attention.
   - A Per-file checks section: for each scanned .md file, include its relative path and report three items: (a) TODO count (case-sensitive occurrences of "TODO:"), (b) missing citation keys (keys referenced in [@key] or [@key1; @key2] that are not present in sources/citations.json), and (c) broken local links (relative link targets found in [text](path) or ![text](path) that do not exist on disk, resolved relative to the Markdown file’s directory). List missing citation keys and broken links explicitly; if none, write "none".
   - An Aggregate totals section listing: total_markdown_files_scanned, total_todos, total_missing_citation_keys (unique across all files), and total_broken_local_links.
   Create the reports/ folder if it doesn’t exist.

2) Edit notes/critique_outline.md in place as follows:
   - Replace the line "Last status: pending" with "Last status: updated".
   - Immediately below the top-level heading, insert a short section titled "Status Update" with exactly three bullet points summarizing this file’s results from your scan: (1) "TODOs: X" with the numeric count, (2) "Missing citations: ..." listing keys or "none", and (3) "Broken local links: ..." listing paths or "none".

Do not use the internet. All counts and lists must be derived from the local files so results can be recomputed and verified.

Inputs are under the workspace as provided below; outputs must be exactly reports/project_status_ancient_japan.md and the modified notes/critique_outline.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
